research_question: When SPY is in a causally observable daily drawdown state, does holding GLD for the next common-session interval produce positive net performance and preserve capital better than holding SPY over the identical stress intervals?
null_hypothesis: The GLD stress sleeve has no positive net-return or capital-preservation advantage over the identically timed SPY stress sleeve.

economic_prior.title: Is Gold a Hedge or a Safe Haven? An Analysis of Stocks, Bonds and Gold
economic_prior.use: Gold may act as a short-lived safe haven in extreme stock-market conditions; this does not validate the frozen threshold or result.

economic_prior.title: Is gold a safe haven? International evidence
economic_prior.use: Gold safe-haven evidence is strongest for developed markets and short-lived extreme shocks; this supplies an economic prior only.

outcome_blind_freeze.monthly_input_check: The unchanged 252-session and -10 percent economic state produced only 4 active validation intervals across 2 years, below the predeclared 12 intervals and 3 years; it was stopped before return access.
outcome_blind_freeze.daily_input_check: With the unchanged economic state evaluated daily, development has 1005 complete and 408 active intervals across 2 active years, validation 2011 complete and 118 active across 4 active years, and holdout 2132 complete and 334 active across 6 active years.
outcome_blind_freeze.adjudication: Daily monitoring is frozen before outcome access because the cited safe-haven prior is short-lived and the daily design independently satisfies a minimum 60 active intervals across 3 years in each gated split. No threshold, asset, cost, split or gate was selected from returns.

input_identity.common_session_rule: Inspect the union inside the SPY/GLD overlap before forming the exact common-session panel; any union date missing either symbol blocks. No forward fill or calendar substitution.
input_identity.row_rule: Every symbol-date key is unique and every required price is finite and strictly positive.
input_identity.minimum_history_rule: At least 252 exact common sessions are required through a daily decision close.
input_identity.consumed_common_session_hash: SHA256 over the exact executable rows using input_hash_serialization
input_identity.input_hash_serialization: UTF-8 with no header; order trade_date ascending then symbol order SPY,GLD; one line per row formatted YYYY-MM-DD|symbol|format(float(raw_open),'.17g')|format(float(adj_open),'.17g')|format(float(adj_close),'.17g') followed by LF; nulls are forbidden

strategy.decision: every exact common SPY/GLD source session at close after the 252-session warm-up
strategy.stress_score: SPY adj_close at decision divided by the maximum SPY adj_close over the latest 252 exact common sessions including the decision, minus one
strategy.stress_condition: SPY adj_close at decision <= 0.90 times the 252-session peak; this is the exact implementation predicate for a drawdown of at least 10 percent and avoids binary floating-point subtraction ambiguity in the reported stress_score
strategy.execution: immediately next exact common SPY/GLD session at open; the following common-session open is the interval endpoint regardless of whether the state changes
strategy.activation_lag: one common-session execution open after the daily close decision
strategy.exit: first subsequent common-session execution open whose immediately preceding daily decision is not stress
strategy.same_state_retention: Consecutive stress sessions retain existing GLD shares without artificial liquidation or repurchase.
strategy.forbidden_features: no Market State Observer, VIX, volatility threshold, momentum ranking, hysteresis, intraday trade, stop loss, take profit, parameter grid, leverage or shorting

accounting.state_exit: sell the full existing position once at the adjusted-open-ratio marked value and retain resulting cash
accounting.split_initialization: Each split starts with USD 40000 cash and acts on its first complete interval target.
accounting.return_and_cost_attribution: At each execution open, first mark the prior position, then execute any state transition and charge each sale or purchase cost exactly once. The execution-open NAV after those trades is recorded. Daily net return is current recorded NAV divided by the previous recorded NAV minus one; the first recorded NAV is divided by USD 40000. Terminal liquidation cost is included in the final recorded NAV.
accounting.terminal_liquidation: sell any remaining position exactly once at the final complete interval endpoint open and record the post-sale NAV; no terminal rebalance
accounting.one_way_turnover: sum of executed notionals divided by account NAV immediately before each trade
accounting.cost_drag: sum of explicit transaction costs in USD divided by initial capital

splits.complete_interval_rule: decision close, next-session entry open and following-session interval endpoint open must all be inside one split; cross-split and terminally incomplete intervals are purged
splits.combined: concatenate validation then holdout daily net-return streams without resetting the peak; each split retains its own initial trade and terminal liquidation

comparators.B1_SPY_BUY_HOLD: contextual whole-share SPY bought at first split raw open and liquidated at final split adjusted-open-ratio value
comparators.B2_SPY_STRESS_SLEEVE: identical stress decisions and cash-outside rule but hold SPY instead of GLD
comparators.B3_GLD_BUY_HOLD: contextual whole-share GLD bought at first split raw open and liquidated at final split adjusted-open-ratio value
comparators.B4_GLD_STRESS_SAFE_HAVEN: the frozen GLD-in-stress and cash-outside-stress sleeve

lineage_distinction.US31: Rejected US31 is fixed 50/50 SPY/GLD; this strategy is an episodic GLD/cash sleeve driven by a causal SPY drawdown state.
lineage_distinction.US46: Rejected US46 is fixed 50/50 QQQ/GLD; QQQ is absent here and US46 remains closed.
lineage_distinction.dual_momentum: Merged dual momentum ranks SPY, QQQ and GLD by 252-session return; this strategy performs no ranking or positive-momentum selection.
lineage_distinction.inverse_volatility: Merged inverse volatility continuously allocates across SPY, QQQ and GLD; this strategy holds only GLD or cash.
lineage_distinction.spy_200d_trend_cash: The closed retrospective baseline uses a 200-session moving-average trend rule; this strategy instead freezes a 252-session peak drawdown of at least 10 percent.

metric_formulas.cagr: ending_NAV / 40000 raised to 365.25 / actual_calendar_days, minus 1; nonpositive ending NAV or nonpositive day span fails
metric_formulas.annualized_volatility: sample standard deviation ddof=1 of daily net returns times sqrt(252); fewer than two returns or a nonfinite result fails
metric_formulas.daily_maximum_drawdown: minimum of NAV / running_peak_NAV - 1 over the recorded daily post-trade NAV path beginning from 40000
metric_formulas.calmar: CAGR divided by absolute daily maximum drawdown; zero drawdown yields null and mechanically fails any Calmar gate
metric_formulas.positive_month_fraction: retained for cross-sprint comparability and computed from non-overlapping calendar-month compounded daily net returns
metric_formulas.positive_active_interval_fraction: Label an interval active exactly when its originating decision has stress_condition=true. Its net return is post-trade NAV at the following-session endpoint open divided by post-trade NAV at its entry open minus one. Therefore a GLD-to-cash sale cost at the endpoint belongs to that active interval, while a cash-to-GLD purchase cost at the endpoint belongs to the preceding inactive interval; every cost is counted exactly once. Count strictly positive active-interval returns divided by active_stress_interval_count.
metric_formulas.largest_calendar_year_profit_contributio: maximum positive calendar-year USD PnL divided by the sum of positive calendar-year USD PnL; nonpositive denominator yields null and fails its gate
metric_formulas.whole_share_cash_utilization: purchase raw notional divided by cash available immediately before that purchase; report arithmetic mean across purchases or null if no purchase

inference.series_frequency: complete daily account net returns including zero-return cash intervals and transaction costs under return_and_cost_attribution
tests: validation B4 mean net daily return > 0
validation B4 minus B2 mean net daily return > 0
holdout B4 mean net daily return > 0
holdout B4 minus B2 mean net daily return > 0
inference.prng_stream: Instantiate one NumPy Generator(PCG64(seed)) once and consume it sequentially in tie_order without per-test reinitialization.
inference.resampling: For a length-N centered test series, draw ceil(N/20) block starts uniformly from integers [0,N), take 20 circular observations from each start, concatenate in draw order and truncate to N.
inference.null_centering: For B4>0 subtract B4 observed arithmetic mean. For B4-B2 form paired daily differences first and subtract their observed arithmetic mean.
inference.one_sided_p_value: (1 + count(null bootstrap arithmetic mean >= observed arithmetic mean)) / (20000 + 1)
inference.family_control: Holm FWER; stable sort by raw p then tie_order; rank r from 1 has multiplier 5-r; adjusted_p_r is the running maximum through rank r of min(1,multiplier*raw_p); rank threshold is 0.025/multiplier
inference.simultaneous_lower_bound: For each ranked test, observed mean minus the linearly interpolated quantile at 1-rank_threshold of that test's null bootstrap means.
inference.pass_rule: Every Holm-adjusted p is at most 0.025 and every rank-specific simultaneous lower bound is strictly positive.

primary_gates: minimum active-stress interval and distinct-year coverage passes in validation and holdout
holdout B4 daily maximum drawdown is less severe than B2
holdout B4 Calmar is strictly greater than B2
combined B4 positive active-interval fraction > 50 percent

at 30 bps on the combined validation-plus-holdout path, combined B4 CAGR > 0, combined B4 daily maximum drawdown is less severe than combined B2, and combined B4 largest positive-year contribution < 40 percent
all four Holm-adjusted inference tests pass
zero input, identity, whole-share, cost, database or output failures
stop_rule: After qualification claim exactly one outcome run. Any post-claim failure consumes the run. Do not retune, rerun, substitute data, or alter symbols, stress window, threshold, inequality, timing, retention, costs, splits, inference or gates.
